question_text: A magnet is broken in half. What happens to the broken pieces?

options.label: A
options.text: One piece becomes north only and one piece becomes south only

options.label: B
options.text: Both pieces become complete magnets with their own north and south poles

options.label: C
options.text: The pieces lose all magnetic properties

options.label: D
options.text: The pieces merge back together magnetically

correct_answer: B) Both pieces become complete magnets with their own north and south poles
explanation: When a magnet breaks, each piece still has both a north and south pole. You cannot isolate a single pole because magnetism always exists in pairs.
